meta.note: Sprint 0 is COMPLETED. This document serves as the official Sprint 0 record for audit and approval. All deliverables confirmed present in /root/ems-team/output/docs/.

name: Sprint 0 — Analysis & Architecture
goal: Analyze the legacy MSSQL .bak database, design the target PostgreSQL schema, define all API contracts, and document the solution structure. Zero production code written. All output is documentation for human review before Sprint 1 begins.

stories.title: [ARCHITECT] Analyze MSSQL .bak file and extract legacy schema
stories.description: Mount or restore the 4.4 MB MSSQL backup using Docker SQL Server Developer Edition. Extract all DDL. Document every table, column, FK, and index. Map legacy customers + contact history columns to new schema. Flag PII fields. Map MSSQL types (NVARCHAR, UNIQUEIDENTIFIER, BIT, DATETIME2) to PostgreSQL equivalents (VARCHAR, UUID, BOOLEAN, TIMESTAMPTZ).
acceptance_criteria: ✅ All legacy tables documented with estimated row counts
✅ Column mapping old → new defined for customers and contact history
✅ PII fields flagged (email, phone, address)
✅ MSSQL-specific types mapped to PostgreSQL equivalents
✅ Source row counts confirmed: 639 customers, 892 contact histories

stories.title: [ARCHITECT] Design PostgreSQL target schema
stories.description: Design full PostgreSQL schema for all entities. Base fields on every table: Id(UUID), CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, IsDeleted. Tenant field on every business table: ProjectId(UUID FK). Full entity list: Projects, Users, UserProjectRoles, RefreshTokens, Customers, ContactHistories, CustomerTasks, Pipelines, Opportunities, Notifications, SyncLogs, AuditLogs. All enums, indexes, and FK relationships defined.
tables_designed: Customers (ProjectId, FullName, Email, Phone, Company, ExternalId, SaasSource, Label enum, Status enum, LastContactDate)
ContactHistories (CustomerId, ProjectId, Type enum, Date, Notes, ContactResult enum)
CustomerTasks (CustomerId, ProjectId, AssignedUserId, Title, DueDate, IsCompleted)
Pipelines (CustomerId, ProjectId, PlannedDate, Notes, Status enum)
Opportunities (CustomerId, ProjectId, OwnerId, Title, Value, Stage enum, ExpectedCloseDate)
Notifications (UserId, ProjectId, Type enum, Message, EntityType, EntityId, IsRead)
SyncLogs (ProjectId, SaasSource, Direction, Status, Payload jsonb, RecordCount, AttemptCount, NextRetryAt)
AuditLogs (UserId, ProjectId, EntityType, EntityId, Action, OldValues jsonb, NewValues jsonb)
acceptance_criteria: ✅ All entities have base fields (Id, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, IsDeleted)
✅ Foreign key relationships fully defined
✅ Indexes on ProjectId, Email, ExternalId, CreatedAt DESC
✅ All enum values listed and finalized
✅ Schema validated against legacy migration mapping

stories.title: [ARCHITECT] Define API contracts and OpenAPI spec
stories.description: Define all REST endpoints: Auth, SuperAdmin, Customers, ContactHistory, Notes, Tasks, Opportunities, Sync, Dashboard, Reports. Specify request/response schemas, required roles, error codes. Define SaaS A and SaaS B inbound payload formats. Define outbound callback payload format to SaaS.
acceptance_criteria: ✅ All endpoints listed with method, path, auth requirement, allowed roles
✅ Request/response body schemas defined
✅ Standardized error codes documented (401, 403, 404, 422, 500)
✅ Sync payload formats for SaaS A, SaaS B, and outbound callbacks defined
✅ ApiResponse<T> wrapper format documented
✅ Base URLs for dev and prod specified

stories.title: [ARCHITECT] Define solution folder structure and project scaffold plan
stories.description: Document the exact .NET solution folder structure (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, API, Tests), frontend folder structure, CI/CD workflow layout, and environment variable template. The actual scaffold was created as part of Sprint 0 output.

solution_structure.src/IonCrm.Domain: Entities, Enums, Domain Events — no external dependencies
solution_structure.src/IonCrm.Infrastructure: EF Core DbContext, Neon PostgreSQL, Repository implementations, External service clients
solution_structure.src/IonCrm.API: ASP.NET Core 8 Web API — Controllers, Middleware (Tenant, Auth, GlobalException), Program.cs

di_strategy.Application: MediatR, FluentValidation registered in IServiceCollection extension
di_strategy.Infrastructure: EF Core DbContext, Repository implementations, HttpClients registered
di_strategy.API: Controllers, Middleware, JWT Bearer, CORS, Rate Limiting, Health Checks

acceptance_criteria: ✅ Every project layer mapped to Clean Architecture purpose
✅ DI registration strategy described per layer
✅ IonCrm.sln created with all 5 projects
✅ .env.example contains all required variable names (no real values)

architecture_decisions.decision: PostgreSQL via Neon (serverless) for both dev and prod
architecture_decisions.rationale: No server management, scales to zero, Railway-compatible, free tier sufficient for MVP
Avoids enumeration attacks, compatible with multi-source sync (ExternalId from SaaS can coexist)

architecture_decisions.decision: Soft deletes on all entities (IsDeleted boolean)
architecture_decisions.rationale: Audit trail preservation, SaaS sync re-creation safety (re-activate instead of re-insert)

architecture_decisions.decision: ProjectId on every tenant entity (not schema-per-tenant)
architecture_decisions.rationale: Simpler ops, single DB, row-level isolation via TenantMiddleware — sufficient for MVP scale
Clean separation of reads and writes, built-in pipeline behaviors (validation, logging)

architecture_decisions.decision: Background sync via IHostedService (not Hangfire for now)
architecture_decisions.rationale: Zero additional infrastructure; Hangfire can replace if persistence/retry dashboard needed in future

risks.risk: MSSQL .bak file required full SQL Server to restore
risks.mitigation: Used SQL Server Developer Edition Docker image (free for dev); confirmed row counts
ION-001 full discovery completed first; mapping validated before schema design

risks.risk: SaaS A and SaaS B payload formats unknown
risks.mitigation: API contracts defined with placeholder schemas; finalized when credentials provided in Sprint 3
risks.status: ⚠️ Partially open — creds needed for Sprint 3

next_sprint.name: Sprint 1 — Foundation
next_sprint.note: Sprint 1 is done. Dev environments live. 639 customers migrated. Sprint 2 is currently ACTIVE.

current_sprint.name: Sprint 2 — Customer Core Enhancement
current_sprint.next_action: Frontend Agent should begin ION-015, ION-016. Backend Agent should continue ION-018 → ION-020 → ION-024.

approval_gate: Sprint 0 is COMPLETED and this document serves as the official record. Sprint 2 completion approval is the NEXT required gate before Sprint 3 begins.
status: COMPLETED — NO FURTHER APPROVAL REQUIRED FOR SPRINT 0
